'''Adrian Antonio Gardner''' (born 24 June 1983) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who spent the vast majority of his career with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was an [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain representative]] {{rlp|wg}}er.

He began his career at his home town club, [[Barrow Raiders]], and after 11 years at St Helens, ended his playing days with a season loan move to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Early and personal life==
Born in Barrow-in-Furness, Gardner is the elder brother of ex-[[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] {{rlp|wg}}, [[Mat Gardner]]. He is of Brazilian descent through his mother.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/27996607|title=Hull KR: Ade Gardner eyes 2016 Olympics with Brazil sevens team|work=[[BBC News]]|date=24 June 2014}}</ref>
Gardner attended [[St. Bernard's Catholic High School]] in Barrow,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nwemail.co.uk/news/barrow/16447315.keep-it-old-school-with-chance-to-buy-your-former-rugby-jerseys-so-st-bernards-high-in-barrow-can-fund-new-kit/|title=Keep it old school with chance to buy your former rugby jerseys so St Bernard's High in Barrow can fund new kit|work=[[The Mail (Cumbria)|The Mail]]|date=20 February 2017}}</ref> and played junior [[rugby league]] for Barrow Island.<ref>{{cite web |title=Barrow boy Gardner welcomes tough Tests ahead after home-town ordeal |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/nov/03/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=21 January 2026 |date=3 November 2007}}</ref>

==Career==
Gardner debuted for his hometown professional club, [[Barrow Raiders]], in July 2000. The following season, he scored 16 tries for the club in the [[Northern Ford Premiership]]. In October 2001, he signed a three-year contract with [[Super League]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Meet the squad |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/other-sport/meet-the-squad-3559749 |website=Liverpool Echo |access-date=21 January 2026 |date=27 February 2002}}</ref>

Gardner made his Super League début in a match against the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|London Broncos]] on 28 March 2002, which St. Helens lost.<ref>{{cite web |title=Broncos in Saints shock |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/1899945.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 January 2026 |date=28 March 2002}}</ref> Having won [[Super League VI]], St. Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Gardner played on the wing in Saints' 38–0 loss. He established himself as a first team regular in 2003 and has now appeared 281 times for St Helens, scoring 167 [[Try (rugby)|tries]]. Gardner played for St Helens on the wing in their [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final victory against [[Huddersfield Giants]]. Gardner also finished the 2006 season as St. Helens leading try scorer with 29 tries in all competitions.

Gardner was named in the Great Britain training squad throughout 2006. He scored on his international début in XXXX Test match against New Zealand on 27 June 2006.

St. Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Gardner played on the wing and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Saints' 26–4 victory. He was a surprise omission from the Great Britain squad for the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Tri-nations.]]
As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Gardner played on the wing and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in Saints' 18–14 victory.

Gardner was selected in June 2007 in a young Great Britain team named by new coach Tony Smith for their match against France at Headingley. On 25 August, he scored two tries for St. Helens at [[Wembley Stadium]] to help Saints win their 11th Challenge Cup.

He was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for 2008's [[Super League XIII]] season.<ref name="2008 engage Super League Dream Team">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755 |title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team |publisher=Super League |date=15 September 2008 |access-date=26 September 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080918211731/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755 |archive-date=18 September 2008 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

He played in the [[Super League XIII#Grand Final|2008 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081005210219/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|archive-date=5 October 2008|url-status= live}}</ref>

Gardner was selected for the [[England national rugby league team|England squad]] to compete in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] tournament in Australia.<ref name="Purdham earns World Cup call-up">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081009081658/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|archive-date= 9 October 2008 |url-status= live}}</ref> [[2008 Rugby League World Cup Group A|Group A]]'s first match against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] he played on the wing and scored two tries in England's victory.

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

In the 2014 season, Ade Gardner played on loan at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Ade Gardner: St Helens winger joins Hull KR on loan |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/26507379 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 January 2026 |date=9 March 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Ade Gardner extends Hull KR loan deal for the rest of the season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/26853472 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 January 2026 |date=2 April 2014}}</ref> scoring 8 tries in 19 appearances. On 17 May at the [[Docklands Stadium|Etihad Stadium]] during the 2014 Magic Weekend, he scored 2 tries in Hull KR's 38–24 victory against Hull.<ref name="bbc">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27381565|title=Magic Weekend: Hull KR 38–24 Hull FC|work=BBC Sport |access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref>
8 days later at KCOM Craven Park, he scored another 2 tries in Hull KR's 48–16 victory over the London Broncos.<ref name="yahoo">{{cite web|url=https://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/news/rugby-league-rovers-romp-london-win-160932798--spt.html|title=Rugby League – Rovers romp to London win – Yahoo Eurosport UK|publisher=Yahoo!|access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref>

==Retirement==
In November 2014, after returning to St Helens, Gardner announced his retirement from the sport after 15 years as a professional. It had been Gardner's intention to play on till 2015, but accepted an offer to join Saints' backroom staff instead, as an Assistant Strength and Conditioning Coach.<ref name="Ade Gardner Joins St Helens Backroom Staff">{{cite news|url=http://www.totalrl.com/ade-gardner-joins-st-helens-backroom-team|title=Ade Gardner joins St Helens backroom team|date=7 November 2014|first=Aaron|last=Bower|work=Total Rugby League}}</ref>
